edges.description: Absent testicular differentiation leaves dysgenetic streak gonads with little functional steroidogenic tissue.
Failed testis determination leads to female external development despite an XY karyotype.
Failed Sertoli and Leydig differentiation prevents normal fetal AMH and testosterone output.
Absent testosterone prevents masculinization of external genitalia.
Absent functional gonadal tissue causes pubertal estrogen insufficiency.
The structural consequence of failed gonadal differentiation is gonadal dysgenesis.
Failed gonadal differentiation leaves undeveloped streak gonads.
Dysgenetic intra-abdominal gonads with Y-chromosome material confer high risk for gonadoblastoma, dysgerminoma, or germinoma.
Gonadal failure causes absent spontaneous pubertal progression, primary amenorrhea, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.
Dysgenetic gonads may develop gonadoblastoma or invasive germ cell tumors.
The tumor-predisposition mechanism motivates early removal of dysgenetic gonads.

Dysgenetic gonads containing Y-chromosomal material carry elevated lifetime risk of gonadoblastoma and malignant germ cell tumors such as dysgerminoma, driving the recommendation for prophylactic gonadectomy.
edges.target: Gonadoblastoma and germ cell tumor risk

edges.description: Y-containing dysgenetic gonads can develop gonadoblastoma.
Loss of gonadal steroid output causes delayed puberty.
Absent pubertal gonadal hormone production causes primary amenorrhea.
Gonadal failure produces the characteristic hypergonadotropic hypogonadism phenotype.
Pubertal estrogen insufficiency from gonadal failure impairs uterine growth, producing an underdeveloped uterus.
Estrogen insufficiency can delay epiphyseal maturation and contribute to tall stature.

nodes.description: Failure to form functional testes leaves affected 46,XY individuals without normal fetal testosterone and anti-Mullerian hormone production, preventing masculinization and permitting persistence of female internal genital structures.

evidence.reference_title: A rare case of Swyer syndrome from Pakistan in a young girl with primary amenorrhea and 46XY genotype.
evidence.snippet: As a result, there is an absence of testosterone that leads to lack of masculinization and the presence of female genitalia.

evidence.reference_title: A rare case of Swyer syndrome from Pakistan in a young girl with primary amenorrhea and 46XY genotype.
evidence.snippet: The pelvic imaging showed a underdeveloped uterus, along with small ovaries and fallopian tubes.

meta.association: Causal biallelic pathogenic variant in a subset of cases

evidence.reference_title: In vitro functional characterization of the novel DHH mutations p.(Asn337Lysfs*24) and p.(Glu212Lys) associated with gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: In humans, mutations of Desert Hedgehog gene (DHH) have been described in patients with 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis (GD), associated or not with polyneuropathy.

nodes.description: Pubertal progression is delayed or absent because dysgenetic gonads do not provide normal sex-steroid production.

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: The condition usually manifests in teenage and young adults with delayed puberty and primary amenorrhea.

nodes.description: Intra-abdominal dysgenetic gonads that retain Y-chromosome material have a high risk of gonadoblastoma, dysgerminoma, germinoma, or seminoma, making malignancy prevention a central management goal.

evidence.reference_title: Seminoma in 46, XY Gonadal Dysgenesis: Rare Presentation and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: 46, XY GD involves a high risk of gonadoblastoma development with malignant potential such that the onset is greatest at or after the event of puberty.

evidence.reference_title: Late Diagnosis of Swyer Syndrome in a Patient with Bilateral Germ Cell Tumor Treated with a Contraceptive Due to Primary Amenorrhea.
evidence.snippet: Histopathological examination revealed the presence of dysgerminoma in both dysgenetic gonads.

nodes.description: 46,XY complete gonadal dysgenesis arises when critical determinants of testis development such as SRY, NR5A1, or DHH are disrupted, preventing male gonadal differentiation in an XY embryo.

evidence.reference_title: A missense mutation (c.226C>A) in HMG box SRY gene affects nNLS function in 46,XY sex reversal female.
evidence.snippet: The mutation affects down regulation of male sex differentiation pathway and is responsible for 46,XY sex reversal female with gonadal dysgenesis.

evidence.reference_title: In vitro functional characterization of the novel DHH mutations p.(Asn337Lysfs*24) and p.(Glu212Lys) associated with gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: In humans, mutations of Desert Hedgehog gene (DHH) have been described in patients with 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis (GD), associated or not with polyneuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Worldwide cohort study of 46, XY differences/disorders of sex development genetic diagnoses: geographic and ethnic differences in variants.
evidence.snippet: The most commonly mutated genes associated with androgen synthesis and action are AR, SR5A2, and HSD17B3, and the most commonly mutated genes involved in gonadal formation are NR5A1 and MAP3K1.

nodes.description: Affected individuals have a female phenotype despite a 46,XY karyotype.

evidence.reference_title: A missense mutation (c.226C>A) in HMG box SRY gene affects nNLS function in 46,XY sex reversal female.
evidence.snippet: The cytogenetic analysis confirmed 46,XY sex reversal karyotype of a female.

nodes.description: Undeveloped streak gonads are the defining structural abnormality in the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Complete gonadal dysgenesis or Swyer syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by 46,XY karyotype and female phenotype with undeveloped streak gonads and high malignancy risk.

nodes.description: Dysgenetic streak gonads in 46,XY complete gonadal dysgenesis carry a substantially elevated lifetime risk of gonadoblastoma, a germ cell neoplasm that can progress to invasive dysgerminoma. Gonadoblastoma and dysgerminoma are the most commonly found tumors in this condition.

evidence.reference_title: A Risk of Gonadoblastoma in Familial Swyer Syndrome-A Case Report and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: A thorough review of familial CGD with 46,XY DSD in the literature from the last 15 years suggests that the risk of gonadal tumors could be increased in familial compared to sporadic cases (66.6% vs. 15-45%, respectively).

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: The most commonly diagnosed tumors were dysgerminoma and gonadoblastoma.

evidence.reference_title: Malignant Germ Cell Tumors and Their Precursor Gonadal Lesions in Patients with XY-DSD: A Case Series and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Some patients are at higher risk of developing pre-invasive tumors such as germ cell neoplasia in situ (GCNIS) or gonadoblastoma, which is commonly observed in complete or partial dysgenesis

nodes.id: Gonadoblastoma and germ cell tumor risk

nodes.description: Gonadal failure produces low sex-steroid output with compensatory gonadotropin elevation.

evidence.reference_title: Infertility management in a patient with Swyer syndrome: a case report.
evidence.snippet: To highlight the successful use of assisted reproductive technologies in achieving pregnancy and healthy childbirth in a woman with Swyer syndrome, a rare disorder of sex development characterized by gonadal dysgenesis, elevated gonadotropin levels, oestrogen insufficiency, tall stature, delayed puberty, and primary amenorrhoea.

nodes.description: Loss of gonadal hormone production produces delayed puberty, primary amenorrhea, and a hypergonadotropic endocrine pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Therefore, women with amenorrhea and lack of secondary sexual characteristics require careful investigation.

nodes.description: The uterus may be underdeveloped because absent testicular hormones and gonadal steroid insufficiency alter reproductive tract maturation.

evidence.reference_title: A rare case of Swyer syndrome from Pakistan in a young girl with primary amenorrhea and 46XY genotype.
evidence.snippet: The pelvic imaging showed a underdeveloped uterus, along with small ovaries and fallopian tubes.

evidence.reference_title: Worldwide cohort study of 46, XY differences/disorders of sex development genetic diagnoses: geographic and ethnic differences in variants.
evidence.snippet: The most commonly mutated genes associated with androgen synthesis and action are AR, SR5A2, and HSD17B3, and the most commonly mutated genes involved in gonadal formation are NR5A1 and MAP3K1.

meta.association: Causal heterozygous pathogenic variant (autosomal dominant with variable expressivity) in a subset of cases; same gene causes primary ovarian insufficiency in 46,XX individuals

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in NR5A1 associated with ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Mutation of NR5A1 causes 46,XY disorders of sex development, with or without adrenal failure, but growing experimental evidence from studies in mice suggests a key role for this factor in ovarian development and function as well.

nodes.description: Menarche fails to occur because complete gonadal failure prevents normal pubertal maturation.

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: The condition usually manifests in teenage and young adults with delayed puberty and primary amenorrhea.

nodes.description: Early gonadectomy is used to reduce the substantial gonadal malignancy risk associated with dysgenetic streak gonads.

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Gonadectomy was performed in 12 cases (80%).

meta.association: Causal pathogenic variant (typically de novo or Y-linked) accounting for approximately 15% of complete gonadal dysgenesis cases

evidence.reference_title: A missense mutation (c.226C>A) in HMG box SRY gene affects nNLS function in 46,XY sex reversal female.
evidence.snippet: The patient was confirmed with nucleotide substitution (c.226C>A; p.Arg76Ser) at in HMG box domain of SRY gene that causes 46,XY sex reversal female.

nodes.description: Bilateral streak gonads are the defining structural lesion of 46,XY complete gonadal dysgenesis — fibrous, undifferentiated gonadal remnants devoid of functional germ cells or steroidogenic tissue.

evidence.reference_title: A missense mutation (c.226C>A) in HMG box SRY gene affects nNLS function in 46,XY sex reversal female.
evidence.snippet: The clinical phenotypes were hypoplastic uterus with streak gonad and underdeveloped secondary sexual characters.

nodes.description: Dysgenetic streak gonads do not sustain normal pubertal hormone production and create the anatomic substrate for gonadal tumor risk.

evidence.reference_title: Complete gonadal dysgenesis analysis in the population of Latvia: malignant outcomes and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Complete gonadal dysgenesis or Swyer syndrome is a rare genetic disorder characterized by 46,XY karyotype and female phenotype with undeveloped streak gonads and high malignancy risk.

nodes.description: Estrogen insufficiency can delay epiphyseal closure, producing tall stature in untreated affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Infertility management in a patient with Swyer syndrome: a case report.
evidence.snippet: To highlight the successful use of assisted reproductive technologies in achieving pregnancy and healthy childbirth in a woman with Swyer syndrome, a rare disorder of sex development characterized by gonadal dysgenesis, elevated gonadotropin levels, oestrogen insufficiency, tall stature, delayed puberty, and primary amenorrhoea.
